flags.description: The negative equity from your trade-in was not clearly disclosed in your deal. This means you may owe more than expected.
The monthly payment on your contract does not match what the math produces from the stated APR, term, and amount financed. The difference exceeds the acceptable tolerance.
The lease payment does not reconcile with the cap cost, residual, and money factor shown. The variance exceeds the acceptable tolerance.
Credit life or disability insurance was added to your deal. This product is frequently overpriced and rarely the best option for coverage.
The vehicle selling price is higher than the price on your original quote. Review the difference before signing.
The GAP coverage on your deal is priced above the SmartBuyer standard cap for your vehicle price. Consider asking for a reduction or purchasing GAP separately.
The vehicle service contract is priced above the SmartBuyer cap for your mileage band. Ask the dealer to reduce the price or consider third-party options.
An additional GAP charge appears on your captive lender lease. Captive lenders typically include GAP coverage. This additional charge should be reviewed carefully.
Products were bundled without individual prices. You cannot verify what you paid for each product or compare pricing.
A market adjustment or ADM was added above MSRP. This is a fully discretionary dealer charge. It is negotiable.

The total of all backend protection products exceeds the SmartBuyer threshold. Classified as DEALER_CONDUCT: the dealer controls product selection and pricing — excessive backend loading is a conduct choice.
The APR on your contract is higher than the APR on your original quote. This increases your total cost of financing.
A rebate or incentive that appeared on your quote is absent from your contract. Confirm this was intentional.
Backend products do not appear as individual line items — they are only visible as a payment increase.
The Debt Cancellation Agreement is priced above the SmartBuyer cap. DCA pricing is evaluated against the same caps as GAP.
The gross cap cost does not reconcile with the itemized components. There may be an undisclosed charge in your lease.
Products were added to your contract that were not on your original quote. Review each addition.
A rebate or incentive is listed but has not been subtracted from your deal price.
A dealer-controlled fee appears to be mislabeled as a government fee. Government fees are not negotiable — dealer fees may be.
Lease-end protection products were bundled without individual prices. You cannot verify what each product costs.
A trade-in was confirmed but no trade allowance appears in your deal. The trade value was not disclosed.
A trade allowance is listed but does not appear to have been applied to reduce your deal price.
Your monthly payment is higher than it was on your original quote. Review what changed.
Your trade-in allowance is lower in your contract than it was on your quote. This directly increases what you owe.
The total of all front-end add-on products significantly exceeds the SmartBuyer threshold as a percentage of your vehicle price.
A product price changed between your quote and your contract.
The per-mile overage charge for exceeding your mileage allowance is not disclosed in your lease.
A disposition fee is typically charged at lease end if you return the vehicle. It is not disclosed in your lease documents.
The documentary fee exceeds the cap for your state. This fee is largely dealer-controlled and may be negotiable.
A product in your deal does not have an individual price. You cannot verify whether you were charged fairly.
The same product type appears twice in your deal. Verify you are not paying for duplicate coverage.
The maintenance plan is priced above the SmartBuyer cap. Consider whether this plan fits your driving needs.
The tire and wheel protection product is significantly overpriced based on the SmartBuyer cap.
The same government fee appears to be charged twice. Government fees should only appear once.
The acquisition fee on your lease significantly exceeds the standard threshold.
An add-on product is priced above the SmartBuyer individual cap for that product type.
No GAP coverage is present on a deal with significant financial risk. If your vehicle is totaled, you could owe more than the insurance payout.
The vehicle price is above the market range for comparable vehicles in your area based on current listings.
No GAP coverage on a long-term loan. If the vehicle is totaled before significant equity builds, you may owe more than the insurance payout.
Your loan term is 84 months or longer. Extremely long terms significantly increase total interest paid and extend your financial risk period.
The loan-to-value ratio on this deal is elevated. You are financing significantly more than the vehicle is worth.
No vehicle service contract on a high-mileage used vehicle with a long loan term. Repair risk is elevated.
Your lease mileage allowance is below 10,000 miles per year. Exceeding it could result in significant overage charges at lease end.
The residual value on your standard lease program is below the SmartBuyer benchmark. This may affect your total lease cost.
The loan term changed between your quote and your contract. A longer term increases total interest paid.
Your trade-in has negative equity that was rolled into this deal. This increases the total amount you owe.
The trade payoff amount appears conflicted or unclear in your deal documents.
The down payment changed between your quote and your contract.
Your loan term is between 73 and 83 months. Longer terms mean more interest paid over the life of the loan.
Multiple lease-end protection products may overlap in coverage. Review each product to avoid paying for duplicate protection.
A finance certificate or OEM incentive may require using the captive lender. You may have forfeited a cash alternative.
The trade-in allowance may be below market range. Consider getting an independent appraisal before signing.
The monthly payment is slightly above what the stated APR, term, and financed amount produce. The difference is within the review range.
The proposed payment is higher than the math produces. This may indicate undisclosed products in the payment.
The combined total of front-end add-on products exceeds the SmartBuyer soft threshold.
The documentary fee is above average but below the hard cap. It may be worth asking about.
A trade payoff conflict prevents calculation of your net trade position.
The monthly payment matches the math from the stated APR, term, and amount financed.
The lease payment reconciles within tolerance with the cap cost, residual, and money factor.
GAP coverage is included in your captive lender lease at no additional charge.
The total backend protection products are within the SmartBuyer fair threshold.
The GAP coverage is priced within the SmartBuyer fair cap.
The vehicle service contract is priced within the SmartBuyer fair cap for your mileage band.
The maintenance plan is priced within the SmartBuyer fair cap.
The tire and wheel protection is priced within the SmartBuyer fair cap.
The documentary fee is within the SmartBuyer cap for your state.
The lease acquisition fee is within the standard threshold.
The disposition fee is clearly disclosed in your lease agreement.
Your lease mileage allowance is within the standard 10,000-12,000 miles per year range.
The disposition fee was waived as part of a loyalty or pull-ahead program.
The residual value is within the fair range for your standard lease program.
The documentary fee is noted for your review.
A maintenance plan on a new vehicle may overlap with factory warranty coverage. Review what the plan adds beyond your warranty.
The acquisition fee is slightly above standard but below the excessive threshold.
The disposition fee is above standard but disclosed.
A 0% or subsidized APR is present, indicating a manufacturer-supported rate incentive.
No lender, APR, or financed amount detected. This appears to be a cash purchase.
A Debt Cancellation Agreement is present. DCA functions similarly to GAP but has different regulatory treatment.
A DCA is present on a deal with negative equity. DCA satisfies the GAP requirement in this case.
No GAP on a deal with a low down payment. Consider whether GAP protection is appropriate.
No vehicle service contract on a loan with a term over 60 months. Consider whether coverage is appropriate for your situation.
No GAP on a non-captive lender lease. Consider whether GAP protection is appropriate.
A conditional rebate or incentive is present. Verify the conditions that must be met to receive it.
A trade payoff was expected but is not visible in your deal documents.
This lease uses a relatively high residual value, which lowers your monthly payment but may reduce your flexibility to purchase at lease end.
The vehicle may be worth more than the buyout price at lease end. You may have purchase equity.
The vehicle may be worth less than the buyout price at lease end. Purchasing the vehicle may not be the best financial decision.
Your lease has an extended mileage allowance above 12,000 miles per year. Residual fairness is advisory only for non-standard programs.
This appears to be a sign-and-drive lease structure with no cap cost reduction. Fees and taxes are rolled into the cap cost.
A loyalty rebate or disposition waiver was applied as a cap cost reduction.
This is a single-payment lease. All payments are made upfront, reducing the rent charge but carrying risk if the vehicle is totaled early.
Your lease term is outside the standard 24-36 month range. Residual fairness analysis is advisory only for non-standard terms.
Minor lease payment variance within review range.
GAP is charged but the lessor type cannot be confirmed. Verify with your lender whether GAP is already included.
Non-standard mileage program. Residual fairness analysis suppressed.
The residual is slightly below the SmartBuyer benchmark for a standard lease program.
The APR is in the above-benchmark band. Rate scoring is handled by the backend scoring engine.
Tax method noted. No penalty applied. Tax treatment varies by state and lender.
The tire and wheel protection is above average but below the excessive threshold.
Excess mileage product detected in lease. Included in backend total.
Wear and tear protection product detected. Included in backend total.
Multiple Security Deposits detected. Payment math and money factor checks suppressed.
APR confidence is below threshold. Payment math and rate checks suppressed.
Lease payment math skipped due to missing required fields.
MSRP unavailable — sale_price used as proxy for cap calculations (GAP/VSC/Maintenance). Caps are conservative. msrp_source set to sale_price_fallback.
Lease math fields below confidence threshold. Routed to member confirmation.
Audit confidence below threshold. Member routed to Edit Deal Data for field confirmation.
This audit matches a prior submission. Excluded from Trust Score calculations.
A similar prior submission was found with different amounts. Flagged for review.
First audit for this dealer. Trust Score initialized.
One or more Tier 2 fields are missing. Dependent audit steps suppressed.
Fewer than 8 market comparables found. Market price position cannot be determined.
Internal lender analytics flag. Multiple compound risk factors present. Not consumer-facing.
Internal lender analytics flag. Multiple compound lease risk factors present.
A line item could not be classified by the OCR keyword dictionary.
Audit halted. A required field confidence is below threshold. Member prompted to resubmit.
No statutory cap for this state. SmartBuyer benchmark applied.
